DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-22-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Jacob Harker telephone number 415-624-7602 for Plaintiffs the Carpers.
Jay Ramsey of Sheppard, Mullin, Richter & Hampton, LLP telephone number 310-228-3700 for Defendant Flex Marketing Group LLC.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT FLEX MARKETING GROUP LLC'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT is overruled. The current demurrer is an impermissible successive demurrer. Judge Goldsmith's order on the demurrer to the first amended complaint resolved all issues raised by this demurrer adversely to Flex Marketing. By granting leave to amend to identify the terms in the "from" line, Judge Goldsmith rejected all other arguments raised in the prior demurrer, including the very same arguments made in this demurrer based on identifiability of the sender, standing and statute of limitations. Therefore, any demurrer to the second amended complaint was necessarily limited to addressing whether the new allegations adequately identified the terms in the "from" line, yet Flex Marketing made no such argument in this demurrer. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Defendant Flex Marketing Group Llc'S Demurrer To 1st Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jan-13-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Jacob Harker of Law Offices of Jacob Harker telephone number 415-624-7602 for Plaintiffs.
Jay Ramsey of Sheppard Mullin LLP telephone number 310-228-3700 for Defendant Flex Marketing.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Flex Marketing Group LLC'S Demurrer To The First Amended Complaint is sustained with leave to amend. A pleading must allege facts and not conclusions. (Ankeny v. Lockhead Missles & Space Co., (1979) 88 Cal.App.3d 531, 537.) Here, Plaintiffs merely allege that the from line included generic terms. Plaintiffs do not identify the names or terms which were provided in the from line. Thus, the demurrer is sustained, with fifteen days leave to amend, to provide Plaintiffs with an opportunity to identify the terms in the from line. Plaintiffs have standing to bring the complaint as recipients of unsolicited commercial e-mail advertisement. (FAC paragraph 38.) Also, the complaint is not barred by the statute of limitations. Plaintiffs alleged in the original complaint that they did not know FLEX's true name. Defendant's claim that Plaintiffs knew FLEX's identity because the link that was included in the email, when clicked, directed Plaintiffs to FLEX's website, is based on facts which were not alleged in the original complaint and assumes that at the point Plaintiffs filed the complaint, they knew definitively that the link included in the email was from the sender of the email. Additionally, the FAC does not admit that the sender of the emails was readily identifiable from the body of the email. Rosolowski v. Guthy-Renker may distinguishable from the case at hand. (Rosolowski v. Guthy-Renker (2014) 230. Cal.App.4th 1403.) Here, as currently pled, the complaint alleges that the sender's identity was only revealed once the recipient clicked the link included in the body of the email. In Guthy, however, the senders or from names were all well known brands which enabled the recipient to readily ascertain the sender's identity, based on the body of the email. (Guthy 230 Cal.App.4th at 1416.) Therefore, the holding in Guthy does not require the court to sustain the demurrer without leave to amend. Order signed in open Court.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or In The Alternative For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Feb-09-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Attorney TIMOTHY J. WALTON of Walton Twu, LLP, Tel No. (831) 685-9800, and attorney JACOB HARKER of the Law Office of Jacob Harker, Tel No. (415) 624-7602 appearing for Plaintiffs Shelly Carper and Steve Carper;
Attorney DEREK A. NEWMAN of Newman Du Wors, LLP, Tel No. (310) 359-8188 appearing for Defendant Adknowledge, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT ADKNOWLEDGE INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is denied. First, Defendant produced no authority for its contention that Plaintiffs' act of sharing access of the subject email account in some way divested them of ownership of the account, and therefore has not negated the "recipient" element of Plaintiffs' claim. Second, Defendant has failed to produce evidence to support its contention that the sender of the emails could be identified in the body of the emails. While the parties do not dispute that the emails contained a link to the sender's website (UMF # 7), in contrast to Rosolowski v. Guthy-Renker LLC (2014) 230 Cal.App.4th 1403, there is no evidence that the sender was identifiable by the link itself, that there was an unsubscribe link in the emails, or that the sender's physical address was contained in the emails. While Defendant asserted that the emails contain these elements, the evidence submitted in support fails to establish the assertion. Thus, Defendant has not shown as a matter of law that it is entitled to judgment. Plaintiffs' claims are not preempted by the federal CAN-SPAM act. (See Hypertouch, Inc. v. ValueClick, Inc. (2011) 192 Cal.App.4th 805, 825, 832-33.) Finally, Defendant has produced insufficient evidence for the Court to determine whether it has practices and procedures in place that meet the statutory standard for a reduction in the maximum damages permitted. PREVAILING PARTY SHALL PREPARE AND SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER TO THE COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Dawn Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
